title: /etc/hostname contains name of pod even for hostNetwork: true
body:
### Issue Description

I have `podman kube play` starting a pod with `hostNetwork: true`. 

Inside the resulting container, I observe that `/etc/hosts` contains the name of the pod, not the `$(hostname)` of the host (as in `hostNetwork`). With Kubernetes, it would be the name of the node, where the pod is running' and with `podman run --network host ...` it would likewise be the name of the host, not the pod (since `hostNetwork: true`).

Is this a feature or a bug? It currently prevents me from porting a workload from Docker Compose v2.19.1 to Podman 4.3.1 (on Debian 12.1). In particular `hostname -f` fails with "hostname: Name or service not known" inside the container. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

(see above)

### Describe the results you received

(see above)

### Describe the results you expected

(see above)

### podman info output

```yaml
(see above)
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

I have not tried this with the latest upstream release, because I am installing Podman from a Debian package. Debian 12.1 currently comes with Podman 4.3.1. I have checked Podman [release notes](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/RELEASE_NOTES.md), though, and these do not suggest that the latest upstream release would behave differently.

#14015 appears similar, but it was fixed in Podman 4.1.0.

comments.body: I would appear to come down to whether [`infraOptions.Hostname = podSpec.PodSpecGen.PodBasicConfig.Hostname`](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/67f03406877c1a0a2e81973329bed32837452bea/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L619) assigns a [non-default value](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/67f03406877c1a0a2e81973329bed32837452bea/pkg/specgen/podspecgen.go#L17-L22) in that particular case.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Confirmed the issue for me today.

Podman does not follow the naming scheme that kubernetes uses for Pods.

```bash
podman --version
podman version 4.5.1

# Alpine Linux
uname -a
Linux s2 6.1.43-0-lts #1-Alpine SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri, 04 Aug 2023 06:44:47 +0000 x86_64 Linux
```

Test manifest: `test-pod.yaml`
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: nginx
spec:
  hostNetwork: true
  containers:
  - name: nginx
    image: nginx
```

Using Kubernetes: `kubectl`
```bash
kubectl apply -f test-pod.yaml
kubectl exec -it nginx -- bash -c hostname
  minikube # the name of the host running kubenetes
```

Using Podman: `podman kube`
```bash
podman kube play test-pod.yaml
podman exec -it nginx-nginx bash -c hostname
  nginx # the name of the pod in metadata
```

The default value for Pod containers does not match the behavior of kubernetes
Care to open a PR to fix?
